market_summary: Risk-on with broadening breadth: SPY +7.71%, QQQ +9.21%, and IWM +12.00% over the 30-day window, all above MA50 and MA200; bonds are flat-to-weak, which fits continued equity leadership.

Current leadership remains concentrated in semicap/AI infrastructure: KLAC, AVGO, TSM, NVDA, and GOOGL all beat SPY on 30d momentum; energy has rolled over short term with XOM and CVX both negative 1m and below MA50.

Go for a small, turnover-efficient rebalance rather than a reshuffle: the cleanest upgrade is replacing the weakest 30-day name, CVX, with AMZN, which offers stronger near-term momentum and better regime fit without raising sector concentration above limits.

thesis_damage_flags.why: Direct pack evidence of thesis erosion: 1m return -9.03%, below MA50, and trailing both SPY (+7.71%) and QQQ (+9.21%) by a wide margin; no verified positive qualitative offset.
Short-term damage flag but not full break: 1m return -6.63% and below MA50 despite still-strong 3m/6m trend; keep reduced weight because medium-term energy trend remains intact.
Defensive ballast remains low alpha for this regime: momentum composite only 4.46% and 1m return +2.37%, materially lagging the tape; retained only as lower-volatility diversifier.

thesis: Catalyst: AMZN enters with strong 30d momentum (+18.93%), positive trend support above MA50/MA200, and a cleaner consumer/cloud quality-growth profile for the next 30 days than CVX.
Factor edge: this swaps a negative short-term energy laggard for a large-cap quality compounder that is outperforming SPY and fits the current risk-on, growth-led regime.

Variant view: the market may still be underpricing a renewed catch-up in mega-cap platform growth outside the most crowded semicap names, while energy mean reversion may fail if commodity momentum keeps cooling.
risks: Macro: a sudden rates spike or growth scare could hit long-duration growth leaders and favor a rebound in energy/value.
Idiosyncratic: AMZN execution, cloud demand, or retail margin commentary could disappoint; CVX could recover quickly on any oil/geopolitical shock.
Execution/liquidity: although both are highly liquid, replacing after a strong AMZN run risks short-term entry extension if leadership rotates in the next 1-2 weeks.
falsifiable_checks: Over the next 10 trading days, AMZN should hold above its MA50 and outperform CVX on relative return; failure on both is a thesis warning.
By the next weekly rebalance, if XOM and CVX both reclaim MA50 while AMZN underperforms SPY over the same window, reassess the growth-over-energy rotation call.
trade_of_the_day.why_now: Base: breadth is improving and growth/semicap leadership remains intact, so rotating out of the weakest current holding into a stronger trend should add expected 30-day alpha. Bull: AMZN's recent strength broadens the portfolio beyond pure semicap while still leaning into the winning regime. Bear: energy could snap back on commodity or geopolitical shocks, but CVX's current score deficit versus AMZN is wide enough that the expected edge clears normal weekly turnover drag for a paper portfolio.

constraints_check.notes: Appendix: factor scores strongest KLAC/AVGO/TSM/GOOGL/NVDA; AMZN outranks CVX on 30d momentum and regime fit; XOM retained but cut due to short-term breakdown, COST retained as ballast despite weak alpha. Risk budget: IT 53%, Energy 12%, Comm Services 14%, Consumer 21%; no crypto; single-name max 15%. Scenario check: equity index shock hurts semis/growth most, partly offset by COST/XOM; rates shock is a moderate headwind to growth; commodity shock now less concentrated after CVX exit; USD shock manageable with TSM exposure kept at 12%. Execution: one-for-one weekly rebalance only, no additional turnover. Monitoring/kill-switch: review sooner if AMZN loses MA50, if CVX reclaims MA50 with relative strength, or if 30d breadth deteriorates enough that SPY or QQQ lose MA50 support.
